content.text:
{
  "dimension": "anti-pattern-avoidance",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "The coach largely avoided the major AoA anti-patterns and reached the false-end question beautifully by turn 8/10, leading to a real breakthrough. However, turn 2 trips a mild version of 'dont-use-breath-to-repress': the coach opens with a prescribed 4-4/4-6 box breath before any contact or slowing has happened together — using breath as a technique to manage the overwhelm rather than meeting it. The client even names that the breathing 'doesn't work' in turn 3, which is a signal the move was off. The coach recovers well and does not repeat the error, declines the bait to strategize about the list (avoiding fixing-people), does not reflect 'you create your overwhelm' (avoiding the audit-resolver trap), does not match the list-recitation pace, and does not evangelize methodology. Because one mild anti-pattern tripped early but was course-corrected and did not damage the arc, this lands at 2 rather than 3.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Prescribes a structured 4-4/4-6 breath as opening move before any relational contact — mild dont-use-breath-to-repress / technique-without-state."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Refuses to strategize the list; gently questions the 'it always calms down' story without arguing."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Pivots from to-do strategy to the underlying fear — avoids fixing-people."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Classic false-end question ('what happens if you drop the ball') — exactly the expected territory."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Follows the somatic shift rather than the content; doesn't add overwhelm-about-the-overwhelm."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Invites staying with the felt sense, explicitly 'not fix it' — avoids loving-to-transform and fighting the voice."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["meet-resistance-with-okay", "what-are-you-defending", "feeling-the-unfelt-emotion", "overwhelm"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["the-rush-signature", "calibrate-the-threshold"],
  "notes": "The breath prescription in turn 2 is the only clear slip; everything after is well-executed avoidance, including declining the client's repeated baits to strategize."
}
